nodes.name: Source
nodes.description: The one live input: TrueDepth front camera, LiDAR back camera, an imported depth+color clip, or a still. Auto mode picks the best available and falls back gracefully.
nodes.exampleUse: Set mode to trueDepth and feed Depth into Depth Drive so your face pushes pins out of the wall.

nodes.name: Clip Transport
nodes.description: Play, pause, speed and loop-range control for imported 30fps depth+color clips. Position output reports the playhead 0-1; looped fires each wrap.
nodes.exampleUse: Drive speed from an LFO between 0.25 and 2 so a baked dance clip breathes in slow motion.

nodes.name: Still Image
nodes.description: An imported photo (with its baked depth map if available) as a static source. Great for portraits that you animate with Move nodes instead of live motion.
nodes.exampleUse: Load a portrait, sample Depth and Video Color, then add Ripple displacement so the frozen face shimmers.

nodes.name: Depth
nodes.description: Samples raw sensor depth at each pin and remaps the near-far range to 0 (far) through 1 (near). No ML, just the sensor.
nodes.exampleUse: Depth into Depth Drive is the core patch: the closer you lean in, the further pins push toward the camera.

nodes.name: Video Color
nodes.description: Samples the source's color video at each pin, turning the pin wall into a living mosaic of the camera image.
nodes.exampleUse: Video Color into Pin Color gives an instant camera-mirror; add Posterize for a print-poster look.

nodes.name: Confidence
nodes.description: Per-pin sensor confidence, 0-1. Use it to fade or hide pins where the depth data is noisy, like hair edges and glass.
nodes.exampleUse: Multiply Depth by Confidence before Depth Drive so flickery edge pins stay calm.

nodes.name: Proximity
nodes.description: One control signal for how close the nearest (or average) subject is, 0 far to 1 touching. Fires a trigger when someone crosses the threshold.
nodes.exampleUse: Wire entered to a Shockwave so walking up to the screen detonates a ring of pins.

nodes.name: Domain
nodes.description: The pin lattice itself: up to 307,200 sites, corners pinned to the 3:4 view. Every topology is a warp of one canonical grid, so the morph input crossfades layouts per pin with no re-allocation.
nodes.exampleUse: Set rect to spiral and sweep morph with a slow LFO so the whole wall coils and uncoils.

nodes.name: Jitter
nodes.description: Nudges each pin's home position by a seeded per-pin random offset, breaking machine-perfect regularity.
nodes.exampleUse: A touch of gaussian jitter makes a rect grid read as hand-placed instead of printed.

nodes.name: Rect Region
nodes.description: A soft-edged rectangular mask in view space: 1 inside, 0 outside. Feed it anywhere a field goes to confine an effect to a zone.
nodes.exampleUse: Multiply a Ripple by a Rect Region so waves only run across the bottom third of the wall.

nodes.name: Ellipse Region
nodes.description: A soft elliptical mask in view space, 1 inside fading to 0 at the edge. The go-to spotlight shape.
nodes.exampleUse: Drive the ellipse center from Hand Position so a mask of raised pins follows your palm.

nodes.name: UV Transform
nodes.description: Pans, zooms and rotates the UV that each pin uses to sample the Source, without moving the pins themselves.
nodes.exampleUse: Slowly scroll offset.x so a still portrait drifts across the pin wall like a panning camera.

nodes.name: Grid Info
nodes.description: Per-pin constants from the active Domain: grid UV (xy of a vec3), normalized index 0-1, and distance from view center. The raw material for gradients and sweeps.
nodes.exampleUse: Send uv through Sine into Size for perfect vertical stripes that need no texture at all.

nodes.name: Edge Policy
nodes.description: Decides what happens at the frame border when warps push pins outward: let them fly, fade their size to zero, or clamp them to the edge.
nodes.exampleUse: Use fade with a wide margin so a spiral morph dissolves gracefully at the frame instead of stacking pins on the border.

nodes.name: Shape
nodes.description: Sets the cap mesh for every pin: one of eight instanced 3D shapes with matched LOD meshes.
nodes.exampleUse: Switch from sphere to spike and the same depth patch instantly reads as a bed of nails.

nodes.name: Shape Morph
nodes.description: Per-pin crossfade between two cap shapes. Meshes are vertex-corresponded, so blend is a smooth geometric lerp, not a swap.
nodes.exampleUse: Drive blend with a Body Region mask so pins over your torso are spikes while the background stays spheres.

nodes.name: Size
nodes.description: Scales each pin's cap uniformly: final size is base times the size field, clamped between min and max. Size 0 hides a pin.
nodes.exampleUse: Feed audio bass through Envelope into size so the whole wall swells on every kick.

nodes.name: Stretch
nodes.description: Per-axis, per-pin scaling of the cap shape. Turns tubes into needles, slabs into louvers, discs into blades.
nodes.exampleUse: Stretch slabs along Z by the Velocity field so fast-moving pins smear into motion streaks.

nodes.name: Rotation
nodes.description: Sets each pin's orientation from a per-pin euler field (in turns). Static tilt patterns, not continuous spinning.
nodes.exampleUse: Rotate cones by Grid Info uv so they fan outward from the center like a sunflower head.

nodes.name: Spin
nodes.description: Continuously rotates each pin around one axis at a per-pin rate in turns per second. Each pin accumulates its own angle, so modulating rate never causes jumps.
nodes.exampleUse: Spin rings at a rate driven by Pinch Amount: close your fingers and the whole wall whirs to a stop.

nodes.name: Look At
nodes.description: Orients every pin to face a point in view space, computed analytically per pin. Amount blends between resting orientation and full aim.
nodes.exampleUse: Point cones at Hand Position so ten thousand pins track your fingertip like iron filings.

nodes.name: Stem
nodes.description: Extrudes a stem from the Z=0 wall up to each cap, with per-pin thickness and color fields. Taper thins the stem toward the cap.
nodes.exampleUse: Blade-profile stems with thickness driven by Depth turn a face scan into a field of grass leaning out of the wall.

nodes.name: Material
nodes.description: Chooses how pins are shaded: flat unlit color, lit by the Stage lights, or a matcap for instant studio looks.
nodes.exampleUse: Matcap chrome plus a dark Background makes every depth patch look like liquid metal.

nodes.name: Hide
nodes.description: Shows or hides pins by a mask field: hard cutoff at a threshold, or smooth fade where size follows the mask.
nodes.exampleUse: Hide with the background Body Region so only the person remains, floating as pins in the dark.

nodes.name: Displace
nodes.description: Adds a per-pin XYZ offset to pin positions. The universal motion input: anything that makes a vec3 field can move the wall.
nodes.exampleUse: Splat a Ripple into offset.z to roll rings of raised pins out from a touch point.

nodes.name: Depth Drive
nodes.description: The signature move: pulls pins off the flat wall toward the camera by the depth field (Z = depth to the gamma, times gain). Holes in the data retract flush to zero.
nodes.exampleUse: Depth into Depth Drive at gain 1.5 makes your silhouette bulge out of the wall in true relief.

nodes.name: Spring
nodes.description: Physically springs each pin toward wherever the chain upstream says it should be. Six floats of state per pin (3D displacement plus velocity) give real overshoot and wobble.
nodes.exampleUse: Put Spring after Depth Drive with low damping so every gesture leaves the wall jiggling like jelly.

nodes.name: Lag
nodes.description: Exponentially smooths any per-pin float field over time with one float of state per pin. The cheapest way to calm a jittery field.
nodes.exampleUse: Lag the raw Depth field before Depth Drive to melt sensor noise into syrupy motion.

nodes.name: Trail
nodes.description: Peak-hold with decay: the output snaps up instantly with the input but falls back slowly, leaving glowing trails where things moved. Two floats of state per pin.
nodes.exampleUse: Trail on a Body Region mask leaves a fading afterimage of raised pins wherever you dance.

nodes.name: Echo
nodes.description: A soft delayed copy of a field, approximated by two cascaded smoothers (2 floats per pin), with feedback for repeating ghosts. Not a true frame delay, but it sounds like one.
nodes.exampleUse: Echo a hand-driven mask and Displace with both copies to get a ghost hand chasing the real one.

nodes.name: Velocity
nodes.description: Measures how fast a per-pin field is changing (previous value plus smoothed rate, 2 floats per pin). Speed is absolute, signed keeps direction.
nodes.exampleUse: Velocity of the Depth field into Velocity Color makes only the moving parts of a body burn hot.

nodes.name: Ripple
nodes.description: Endless analytic rings radiating from a point: pure distance-from-center math evaluated per pin, so it costs nothing and never desyncs. Fully allowed in v1 because no neighbor reads happen.
nodes.exampleUse: Center on Touch slot 1 and Displace Z by height so taps drop pebbles into the pin pond.

nodes.name: Shockwave
nodes.description: A single expanding ring per trigger, computed analytically from the trigger time (up to 4 overlapping waves, zero per-pin state). Sharp, synchronized, and cheap.
nodes.exampleUse: Fire from Beat and Displace Z by pulse so every kick drum detonates outward from the center.

nodes.name: Scatter
nodes.description: Throws each pin along its own seeded random direction (or away from a point) by the amount field. Stateless: amount 0 always returns pins exactly home.
nodes.exampleUse: Ramp amount with an Envelope on Burst so loud moments explode the image into confetti that reassembles.

nodes.name: Twist
nodes.description: Rotates pin positions around a center in the view plane, with the angle scaled by distance for whirlpool curls.
nodes.exampleUse: Drive angle from Head Pose roll so tilting your head literally wrings the picture.

nodes.name: Freeze
nodes.description: While hold is up, each pin keeps the position it had at the moment of capture (3 floats per pin). Blend crossfades between frozen and live.
nodes.exampleUse: Gate hold from a fist gesture: clench to freeze a 3D statue of yourself, open to melt back to live.

nodes.name: Palette
nodes.description: Maps any 0-1 field through one of 15 built-in colormaps. Shift scrolls the map for easy animated cycling.
nodes.exampleUse: Depth into Palette turbo is the classic thermal-camera relief; animate shift with a slow LFO to make it breathe.

nodes.name: Pin Color
nodes.description: Binds a color field to the pin caps. The last Pin Color in the chain wins, so stack them with masks for layered looks.
nodes.exampleUse: Video Color into Pin Color for a mirror; add a second Pin Color masked by a Body Region for a tinted subject.

nodes.name: Tint
nodes.description: Multiplies a color field by a single color, with an amount blend. Fast global mood control.
nodes.exampleUse: Tint the whole Video Color feed amber and drop amount on beat triggers for flashbulb pops of true color.

nodes.name: Depth Gradient
nodes.description: Blends between two colors by the depth field, so nearness paints itself. Simpler than a full Palette when you just want two poles.
nodes.exampleUse: White near, deep blue far: your face emerges from darkness as you lean toward the phone.

nodes.name: HSV Shift
nodes.description: Offsets hue (in turns), saturation and value of a color field. Hue wraps, so a ramp input gives endless rainbow cycling.
nodes.exampleUse: Feed hue from a 0.02 Hz LFO to slowly rotate the entire palette through the spectrum.

nodes.name: Velocity Color
nodes.description: Pushes a color field toward a hot color wherever the speed field is high. Pair with the Velocity node to paint motion.
nodes.exampleUse: Cool gray base, ember-orange hot: only the parts of you that move catch fire.

nodes.name: Color Mix
nodes.description: Blends two color fields per pin by a third field: crossfade, additive glow, or multiply shadowing.
nodes.exampleUse: Mix Video Color with a Palette by a Body Region mask so the person is photographic and the room is synthetic.

nodes.name: Posterize
nodes.description: Quantizes each color channel to a fixed number of steps for screen-print and riso looks.
nodes.exampleUse: Posterize Video Color to 3 levels over a hex Domain for instant halftone poster art.

nodes.name: Strobe
nodes.description: Flashes the whole field to a strobe color on each trigger, decaying back over the set time. The envelope runs at control rate, so it costs no per-pin state.
nodes.exampleUse: Flash from Beat high-band triggers for hi-hat driven white lightning across the wall.

nodes.name: Add
nodes.description: a plus b. Accepts signals or fields on either side; signal inputs broadcast to every pin. Runs at control rate when both inputs are signals.
nodes.exampleUse: Add a Ripple to the Depth field so waves ride on top of the live relief.

nodes.name: Subtract
nodes.description: a minus b, per pin or per signal.
nodes.exampleUse: Subtract a Lagged copy of Depth from raw Depth to isolate just the fresh motion.

nodes.name: Multiply
nodes.description: a times b. The workhorse for masking: multiply any effect field by a 0-1 mask to confine it.
nodes.exampleUse: Multiply a Shockwave by a Body Region so the blast only travels across the person.

nodes.name: Divide
nodes.description: a divided by b, guarded against divide-by-zero (near-zero denominators clamp).
nodes.exampleUse: Divide a field by RMS loudness to make quiet passages exaggerate detail.

nodes.name: Mix
nodes.description: Linear blend from a to b by t, per pin. The crossfader of the patch.
nodes.exampleUse: Mix between two Size recipes with a MIDI fader for a hands-on A/B morph.

nodes.name: Clamp
nodes.description: Pins values into a min-max range. Cheap insurance before Size or Displace.
nodes.exampleUse: Clamp a boosted audio field to 0-1 so bass drops cannot blow pins off screen.

nodes.name: Remap
nodes.description: Rescales one range to another, optionally clamped. The universal unit converter between nodes.
nodes.exampleUse: Remap Pinch Amount 0.2-0.8 to Size 0-2 so the usable finger range covers the full effect.

nodes.name: Curve
nodes.description: Reshapes a 0-1 value through a response curve. Amount blends between linear and the full curve.
nodes.exampleUse: Exponential curve on Depth makes only the closest few centimeters really pop.

nodes.name: Abs
nodes.description: Absolute value, per pin.
nodes.exampleUse: Abs of a signed Velocity gives motion energy regardless of direction.

nodes.name: Min
nodes.description: The smaller of a and b per pin. Min against a mask acts like an intersection.
nodes.exampleUse: Min of two Ellipse Regions carves a lens-shaped active zone.

nodes.name: Max
nodes.description: The larger of a and b per pin. Max of two masks acts like a union.
nodes.exampleUse: Max the left-hand and right-hand Body Regions to treat both hands as one mask.

nodes.name: Quantize
nodes.description: Snaps a continuous field to a fixed number of levels for terraced, stepped looks.
nodes.exampleUse: Quantize Depth to 6 steps before Depth Drive to turn a face into contour-line terraces.

nodes.name: Threshold
nodes.description: Outputs 1 where the input exceeds the level, 0 elsewhere, with hysteresis to stop flicker. When fed a plain signal, the rose trigger fires on each upward crossing.
nodes.exampleUse: Threshold the Depth field at 0.8 to isolate only whatever is nearly touching the screen.

nodes.name: Compare
nodes.description: Compares two values per pin and outputs 1 or 0. Equality uses the tolerance window.
nodes.exampleUse: Compare uv.x > 0.5 to split the wall into two halves running different colors.

nodes.name: Switch
nodes.description: Passes a when select is below 0.5, b when above. Works per pin, so a mask can pick different sources in different zones.
nodes.exampleUse: Switch between a calm and a chaotic Size field using a Toggle flipped by a fist gesture.

nodes.name: Smoothstep
nodes.description: The classic soft step: 0 below edge0, 1 above edge1, silky in between. The nicest way to make masks from continuous fields.
nodes.exampleUse: Smoothstep Depth between 0.6 and 0.9 for a feathered halo around whatever is close.

nodes.name: Sine
nodes.description: Sine waveshaper: out = sin(in times cycles plus phase). Feed it a position field and you get stripes; feed it time and you get oscillation.
nodes.exampleUse: Sine of Grid Info centerDist with animated phase paints concentric breathing rings with zero state.

nodes.name: Power
nodes.description: Raises the input to a power. Above 1 sharpens contrast, below 1 lifts shadows.
nodes.exampleUse: Square a mask to tighten its soft edge into a crisper spotlight.

nodes.name: LFO
nodes.description: The everyday oscillator: five shapes, locked to the BPM transport by division or running free in Hz. Cycle fires once per period.
nodes.exampleUse: A 4-bar sine LFO on Domain morph makes the lattice slowly tide between grid and spiral.

nodes.name: Envelope
nodes.description: Attack-release envelope: each trigger snaps it up over the attack time and lets it fall over the release. The glue between triggers and continuous parameters.
nodes.exampleUse: Beat trigger into Envelope into Size gives the wall a heartbeat instead of a twitch.

nodes.name: Slew
nodes.description: Limits how fast a control signal can rise and fall, with independent times each way. Tames stepped MIDI and jumpy sensors.
nodes.exampleUse: Slew a 7-bit MIDI CC before Size so fader moves glide instead of zipper.

nodes.name: Spring Value
nodes.description: A control-rate spring that chases its target with physical overshoot. Cheaper than the per-pin Spring when one bouncy number is all you need.
nodes.exampleUse: Spring Value on Head Pose yaw makes the Twist angle wobble past where you look, then settle.

nodes.name: Random
nodes.description: A stable per-pin random value, 0-1, hashed from the pin index and seed. Every pin gets its own number that never changes until you reseed.
nodes.exampleUse: Random into Spin rate so every pin whirls at its own speed, forever, deterministically.

nodes.name: Wander
nodes.description: Smooth seeded value-noise that drifts over time, independently per pin, computed analytically with zero state. Organic motion without physics.
nodes.exampleUse: Wander into Displace Z at low amount gives the whole wall a gentle underwater shimmer.

nodes.name: Sample & Hold
nodes.description: Freezes the input field at the instant of each trigger and holds it until the next one. One float of state per pin.
nodes.exampleUse: Sample Wander on every bar so the wall snaps to a new random posture in strict musical time.

nodes.name: Chance
nodes.description: On each trigger, every pin independently rerolls: 1 with the given probability, else 0, held until the next roll. One float per pin.
nodes.exampleUse: Roll on each beat at 0.3 probability and multiply into Size so a random third of the pins punch out per beat.

nodes.name: Audio Levels
nodes.description: Three smoothed 0-1 band levels from the microphone or playing audio: bass, mid, high. The fastest route from music to motion.
nodes.exampleUse: Bass to Size, mid to Bloom amount, high to Grain: a full audio-reactive look in three cables.

nodes.name: Beat
nodes.description: Drum onset detector for one band: low catches kicks, mid catches snares, high catches hats. Strength reports how hard the hit was.
nodes.exampleUse: Low-band onsets into Shockwave: every kick drum slams a ring through the pin wall.

nodes.name: FFT Band
nodes.description: One of the 20 FFT analysis bands as a smoothed signal, for surgical frequency picking.
nodes.exampleUse: Band 17 (air frequencies) into Vignette amount so cymbal shimmer opens up the frame.

nodes.name: Loudness
nodes.description: Overall RMS loudness as one smoothed 0-1 signal. The room-energy meter.
nodes.exampleUse: Loudness into Palette shift so the entire color scheme heats up as the set gets louder.

nodes.name: Burst
nodes.description: Fires on sudden broadband energy jumps: drops, shouts, claps. Amount reports the size of the surprise.
nodes.exampleUse: Burst into Scatter via a fast Envelope so the drop literally blows the image apart.

nodes.name: Spectrum Field
nodes.description: Spreads the 20-band FFT across the pins along a chosen axis, so each column (or ring) of pins tracks its own slice of the spectrum. A per-pin lookup into a 20-value table, no neighbor reads.
nodes.exampleUse: Axis x into Depth Drive turns the whole wall into a giant walk-in spectrum analyzer.

nodes.name: MIDI CC
nodes.description: One MIDI continuous controller as a 0-1 signal, over USB, network or Bluetooth. Channel 0 listens on all channels.
nodes.exampleUse: Map a hardware fader to Depth Drive gain and mix the relief like a channel strip.

nodes.name: MIDI Note
nodes.description: Listens for one note (or any note when set to -1): gate while held, velocity 0-1, a trigger per note-on, and normalized pitch when in any-note mode.
nodes.exampleUse: Any-note pitch into Palette shift so playing up the keyboard sweeps the wall through the colormap.

nodes.name: MIDI Note Range
nodes.description: Turns a keyboard span into a fader: the last note played inside the range maps to position 0-1, with a gate while any range note is held.
nodes.exampleUse: Two octaves mapped to Twist angle: play chords low or high to throw the vortex left or right.

nodes.name: OSC In (Float)
nodes.description: Receives a float on OSC address /points/f/{slot} over the network. Custom address mapping lives in OSC settings.
nodes.exampleUse: Send fader data from TouchOSC on a laptop into Bloom amount for remote front-of-house control.

nodes.name: OSC In (Vec)
nodes.description: Receives 2 or 3 floats on /points/v/{slot} as a vec3 (z is 0 when only two arrive). Perfect for XY pads and tracker data.
nodes.exampleUse: Stream an XY pad into the Ripple center so a remote performer steers the waves.

nodes.name: OSC Trigger
nodes.description: Fires a trigger on any message to /points/t/{slot}. Bang-style events from lighting desks, Max patches or show control.
nodes.exampleUse: Cue a Shockwave from the same OSC bang that fires the venue strobes.

nodes.name: OSC Out
nodes.description: Broadcasts a signal to /points/out/{slot} at up to maxRate Hz, plus an instant message on each send trigger. Points as a controller for everything else in the room.
nodes.exampleUse: Send Proximity nearness out to a lighting rig so house lights dim as people approach the screen.

nodes.name: Body Region
nodes.description: A soft-edged 0-1 mask over one body region, built from 2D body pose. Each pin samples the rasterized region map, no neighbor reads.
nodes.exampleUse: Head region into Size so your head literally swells out of the wall while the room stays flat.

nodes.name: Joint
nodes.description: Raw access to any of the 19 body-pose joints: view-space position, velocity, and tracking confidence.
nodes.exampleUse: Right wrist position into Shockwave center so punches detonate exactly where you strike.

nodes.name: Hand Gesture
nodes.description: Recognizes one of six hand gestures: a trigger when it starts and a gate while it holds. Choose left, right, or either hand.
nodes.exampleUse: Fist begins a Freeze; opening the hand releases the statue back into motion.

nodes.name: Pinch Amount
nodes.description: Continuous thumb-to-index distance as a signal: 0 fingers apart, 1 touching. The most precise body fader in the app.
nodes.exampleUse: Pinch into Domain morph: physically squeeze the lattice from grid to spiral between two fingers.

nodes.name: Hand Openness
nodes.description: How open the hand is, 0 clenched fist to 1 spread palm, as a smooth continuous signal.
nodes.exampleUse: Openness into Scatter amount: clench to gather the pins, open your hand to release them into a cloud.

nodes.name: Hand Position
nodes.description: View-space position of a chosen hand point from the 21-landmark hand skeleton, with a present gate while the hand is tracked.
nodes.exampleUse: Index tip into Ellipse Region center: draw raised-pin circles in the air with one finger.

nodes.name: Face Landmark
nodes.description: Position of any of the 76 face landmarks, addressed by group and index (index -1 gives the group centroid).
nodes.exampleUse: Outer-lips centroid into Ripple center so waves pour out of your mouth as you speak.

nodes.name: Head Pose
nodes.description: Head orientation as three normalized -1 to 1 signals plus view position. Your skull as a three-axis controller.
nodes.exampleUse: Yaw into UV Transform offset.x so the sampled image slides opposite to your glance.

nodes.name: Face Region
nodes.description: A soft mask over the face or a facial feature, rasterized from the 76 landmarks and sampled per pin.
nodes.exampleUse: Mouth mask into Shape Morph blend so your lips are spikes while everything else stays smooth spheres.

nodes.name: Person Present
nodes.description: Fires when a person appears or leaves the frame, with a gate while anyone is tracked. Hold time debounces flicker.
nodes.exampleUse: Entered fires a Shockwave and left fades Size to zero: an installation that wakes for each visitor.

nodes.name: Transport
nodes.description: The global BPM clock every synced node follows. Tap the tap input to set tempo by feel; resync snaps the downbeat to now.
nodes.exampleUse: Tap tempo from a touch slot, then every LFO, Clock and Sequencer in the patch locks to the band.

nodes.name: Clock
nodes.description: A divided pulse from the Transport, with swing. Tick fires each division; phase ramps 0-1 between ticks.
nodes.exampleUse: 1/16 ticks into Chance for a skittering, probabilistic hi-hat of pin flickers.

nodes.name: Step Sequencer
nodes.description: A 16-step value sequencer locked to the Transport; step levels are drawn directly on the node face. Glide slides between steps.
nodes.exampleUse: Sequence Depth Drive gain in 1/8s so the relief pumps a rhythmic pattern even with a still subject.

nodes.name: Counter
nodes.description: Counts incoming triggers, wrapping at the modulo (0 counts forever). Normalized output divides by the modulo for a 0-1 staircase.
nodes.exampleUse: Count bars and wrap at 4 to advance Palette shift every phrase, snapping a new color scheme each time round.

nodes.name: Gate
nodes.description: Passes the value while open is high; otherwise outputs zero or holds the last value. A mute button for any control wire.
nodes.exampleUse: Gate the audio bass feed with a Toggle so one tap silences all audio reactivity for a breakdown.

nodes.name: Delay
nodes.description: Delays a control signal and any triggers by seconds or a beat division. Chain several for canon effects.
nodes.exampleUse: Send the same Beat to two Shockwaves, one delayed a 1/8, for call-and-response rings.

nodes.name: Toggle
nodes.description: Flips between 0 and 1 on each trigger. The patch's light switch; state is control-side, so no per-pin cost.
nodes.exampleUse: Peace-sign gesture flips a Toggle that switches the whole wall between mirror mode and abstract mode.

nodes.name: On Start
nodes.description: Fires exactly once when the patch loads (optionally after a delay). For arming envelopes, seeding counters and opening titles.
nodes.exampleUse: On Start into a slow Envelope on Size so every set begins with the wall blooming from nothing.

nodes.name: Camera
nodes.description: The fixed viewpoint on the pin wall: choose a framing preset, then add only subtle sway and push offsets. The camera never flies; the pins do the moving.
nodes.exampleUse: Feed a whisper of Wander into sway for handheld life without breaking the wall illusion.

nodes.name: Light
nodes.description: A scene light for lit materials; up to two Lights per patch and only one may cast the cheap shadow map. Position it in front of the wall and move it live.
nodes.exampleUse: Drive the light position from Hand Position: hold the sun in your palm and rake shadows across the relief.

nodes.name: Background
nodes.description: What sits behind the pins: a solid, a two-color vertical gradient, or a heavy blur of the source video for ambient context.
nodes.exampleUse: videoBlur behind a sparse scattered Domain makes the pins feel like droplets floating over the real scene.

nodes.name: Bloom
nodes.description: Post-process glow on bright pins. Threshold picks what blooms; radius sets how far the halo spreads.
nodes.exampleUse: Modulate amount with bass so the whole frame breathes light on each kick.

nodes.name: Grain
nodes.description: Film grain over the final frame, static or re-rolled every frame. Hides banding and adds analog warmth.
nodes.exampleUse: A little animated grain over a mono Palette gives instant 16mm documentary texture.

nodes.name: Vignette
nodes.description: Darkens the frame edges to pull the eye center-stage. Roundness morphs between rectangular and circular falloff.
nodes.exampleUse: Snap amount up briefly on Burst triggers for a punch-in feeling without moving the camera.

nodes.name: Render Settings
nodes.description: Global render choices: how overlapping pins blend in depth (solid, additive light-points, or soft glow), overall exposure, and resolution strategy.
nodes.exampleUse: softAdditive with tiny sphere pins turns the wall into a volumetric particle hologram.

nodes.name: Output
nodes.description: The end of every patch: connect your pin chain here and it renders. Exactly one Output per patch.
nodes.exampleUse: Domain into Depth Drive into Pin Color into Output is the smallest complete patch in Points.

nodes.name: Macro
nodes.description: A container that wraps part of your graph into one tidy node with the ports you choose to expose. Double-tap to open and edit the inside; all shipped signature effects are these.
nodes.exampleUse: Collapse your favorite audio-to-size rig into a Macro and reuse it across every patch.

nodes.name: Sticky Note
nodes.description: A text note pinned to the canvas. No ports, no cost; just words for future you.
nodes.exampleUse: Label the performance controls: 'CC1 = intensity, fist = freeze' so stage-you remembers what studio-you built.

nodes.name: Reroute
nodes.description: A pass-through dot for tidying cable runs. Adopts whatever port type is connected, fields included, and compiles to nothing.
nodes.exampleUse: Drop reroutes along a long Depth cable so it hugs the layout instead of crossing forty nodes.

nodes.name: Send
nodes.description: Broadcasts its input to every Receive on the same channel, wirelessly across the canvas. Adopts the connected type, fields included.
nodes.exampleUse: Send the master audio envelope on channel 1 and pick it up in five distant corners of the patch.

nodes.name: Receive
nodes.description: Outputs whatever the matching Send broadcasts, type and all. Outputs silence (zero) when no Send exists yet.
nodes.exampleUse: Receive the beat envelope near your COLOR section without dragging a cable across the whole graph.

nodes.name: Value Display
nodes.description: An inline scope: shows a live number, meter or waveform for signals, and a histogram for fields. Passes its input straight through.
nodes.exampleUse: Drop one after Pinch Amount to check your usable finger range before remapping it.

nodes.name: Pulse Pins
nodes.description: Signature macro: pins puff with the music. Inside: Audio Levels -> Envelope -> Multiply -> Size, fully openable and rewireable.
nodes.exampleUse: Drop it after Depth Drive and the live relief pumps on the kick with zero extra patching.

nodes.name: Wave Field
nodes.description: Signature macro: an analytic ripple rig. Inside: Ripple -> Displace Z, plus an optional Palette tint by wave height. Pure per-pin math, no propagation simulation.
nodes.exampleUse: Park it on a still portrait so slow lacquer waves roll forever across the frozen face.

nodes.name: Body Glow
nodes.description: Signature macro: a chosen body region glows and swells. Inside: Body Region -> Lag -> Color Mix toward the glow color, plus a Size lift. One float of Lag state per pin.
nodes.exampleUse: Torso glow in cyan over a dark mono palette makes a dancer read as a neon ghost in the pin wall.

nodes.name: Beat Shatter
nodes.description: Signature macro: each hit blasts a random subset of pins outward and a spring pulls them home. Inside: Beat -> Envelope -> Chance -> Scatter, with Spring on the return (6 spring floats + 1 chance float per pin). Leave onset unwired to use the built-in low-band Beat.
nodes.exampleUse: On a portrait patch, every snare tears a third of the face into fragments that snap back before the next bar.

nodes.name: Time Stutter
nodes.description: Signature macro: rhythmically gates clip playback between frozen and fast-forward. Inside: Clock -> Toggle -> Gate driving Clip Transport speed between 0 and the catch-up speed.
nodes.exampleUse: Stutter a baked dance clip in 1/8s and the dancer locks and lurches in perfect tempo with the track.

nodes.name: Face Scatter
nodes.description: Signature macro: pins over the face drift apart like a murmuration, then reform. Inside: Face Region -> Scatter driven by Wander noise, with Spring attracting every pin home (6 floats per pin). Purely per-pin home-attraction plus noise; no neighbor reads, so it flocks without a flocking sim.
nodes.exampleUse: Raise amount with Loudness so your face dissolves into swirling birds at the chorus and reassembles in the quiet.

nodes.name: Point Display
nodes.description: Governs how pins hold together. SEPARATION bunches/spreads the grid; VOLUME opens pins outward with depth (point-cloud look); WOBBLE + STABILIZE trade a locked pinout for TDLidar-style sensor wobble (stabilize drives the depth EMA: low=raw/wobbly, high=locked); EDGE LOCK pins the outer ring to the 3:4 frame; GAIN/GAMMA/Z-FLATTEN shape the forward push. Emits pinFieldXY + pinFieldZ VM ops.
nodes.exampleUse: VOLUME 1.5 + STABILIZE 0.15 + EDGE LOCK off = the TDLidar wobbling cloud; SEPARATION 1 + VOLUME 0 + EDGE LOCK on = the locked pin-screen.
